title: The Thirst of Nations: Navigating Global Water Scarcity
subtitle: An examination of how climate change and policy failures are reshaping the world's most vital resource.
description: This series explores the intersection of climate science, international policy, and water management. It provides intermediate learners with a structured look at how water scarcity impacts global stability and local economies.

articles.label: Part 1
articles.note: Defining water stress and the historical context of its acceleration.

articles.label: Part 2
articles.note: The roles of governments, multinational corporations, and local communities.

articles.label: Part 3
articles.note: Recent extreme weather events acting as catalysts for policy change.

articles.label: Part 4
articles.note: The impact of water scarcity on public health, migration, and inequality.

articles.label: Part 5
articles.note: Technological solutions versus systemic policy needs.
